source: Sed, Awk & Grep - Grep command: Context options and include/exclude filters

questions.q: Which option prints NUM lines of context AFTER each match?

options: Print NUM lines of leading context before each match
Stop after the first NUM matching results
questions.why: -m limits how many matches are output.

questions.q: How do you strip blank lines and comment lines from a config file with grep?
questions.why: -v inverts a pattern matching empty lines (^$) or comment lines (^#).

questions.q: What do --include and --exclude control in a recursive grep?
options: Which files (by filename pattern) are searched or skipped
questions.why: They filter which files are searched by name; --exclude-dir skips directories.
